title: Variables and Data Types
summary: Explore variables and different data types in Python.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Variables
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Variables are used to store information that can be referenced and manipulated in a program. In Python, you can create a variable by simply assigning a value to it.

sketchBlocks.title: Data Types in Python
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Python has various data types including integers, floats, strings, and booleans. Each data type serves a different purpose and has its own characteristics.

quizDraft.title: Create a Variable
quizDraft.prompt: Define a variable named `age` and assign it the value of your age.
quizDraft.hint: Use the assignment operator to create the variable.

help.concept: Variables in Python are created by assigning a value to a name using the equals sign.
help.hint_1: Remember to choose a meaningful name for your variable.

quizDraft.title: Identify Data Types
quizDraft.prompt: Create a variable `price` and assign it a float value. Then, create a variable `is_available` and assign it a boolean value.
quizDraft.hint: Make sure to use the correct data types for each variable.

help.concept: Data types in Python include integers, floats, strings, and booleans.
help.hint_1: A float is a number with a decimal point.
help.hint_2: A boolean can be either True or False.

quizDraft.title: Using Strings
quizDraft.prompt: Create a variable `greeting` and assign it a string that says 'Hello, World!'.

help.concept: Strings are sequences of characters used to represent text in Python.
help.hint_1: You can use either single or double quotes for strings.

quizDraft.title: Fill in the Blank: Data Types
quizDraft.prompt: In Python, the data type used to represent true or false values is called ___.

help.concept: Booleans are the data type that can hold True or False values.
help.hint_1: This data type is often used in conditional statements.
help.hint_2: It is named after a mathematician.

quizDraft.template: In Python, the data type used to represent true or false values is called ___.

quizDraft.title: Identify a Data Type
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following is a valid string in Python?
quizDraft.hint: Strings must be enclosed in quotes.

help.concept: Strings in Python are sequences of characters enclosed in quotes.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that uses quotes correctly.
help.hint_2: Remember that both single and double quotes are valid.
